108955.explanation: Nieuw Elan stands for equality of men and women. However, it is also about the 'right person in the right place.'

108956.explanation: Nieuw Elan stands for genuine influence of residents. Therefore, we support a referendum. Major decisions must be broadly supported. Your vote gives us a mandate, but never a blank check. Through participation, citizens' assemblies, and where necessary, via a referendum.

108961.explanation: Alphen aan den Rijn is the heart of the Randstad and historically a hub for distribution by rail, roads, and water. Therefore, Nieuw Elan supports this type of entrepreneurship and businesses.

108966.explanation: Nieuw Elan opposes a fireworks ban. If a new law is actually introduced, Nieuw Elan supports financially backing local initiatives for fireworks shows.

108967.explanation: That is already possible in Alphen aan den Rijn.

108969.explanation: This is not a municipal task. The national government also has various subsidy options for this. However, Nieuw Elan supports assistance with applying for such subsidies.

108971.explanation: The municipality already doubles the state contribution. However, the municipality can provide more room for entrepreneurship of the local broadcaster.

108979.explanation: Nieuw Elan supports adequate protection for enforcement officers.

108980.explanation: Nieuw Elan favors an accessible housing market, especially for starters. However, this must be regulated nationally.

108986.explanation: When people participate, it benefits their social network and helps reduce the distance to the labor market. This requires a tailored approach, as it is not always possible.

108988.explanation: In Nieuw Elan's opinion, it would be better if this went from 120 to 130 percent of the social minimum.

108994.explanation: Care is not faith-based. If private initiatives exist, Nieuw Elan is not against them, but if the (local) government has to pay for that, it is not a good thing.

108995.explanation: The municipality must provide the most appropriate care. Not every 'youth problem' needs to lead to expensive youth care. Sports and culture can also be excellent solutions, supplemented with lighter assistance.

108997.explanation: Nieuw Elan wants the lowest possible municipal taxes for residents. However, we prefer a financially limited increase in property tax over cutting facilities that strengthen our villages, neighborhoods, and associations and provide what Nieuw Elan calls 'the joy of society.'

108998.explanation: Nieuw Elan supports abolishing the dog tax unless it is agreed that its revenue will be reinvested in, for example, off-leash dog areas and similar facilities.

109000.explanation: Nieuw Elan believes there is currently too little space for entrepreneurs and wants to expand opportunities. However, much of this is also controlled by the province, which is not very cooperative. More employment is good for the local economy.

109002.explanation: Nieuw Elan stands for fair and transparent housing allocation: no priority for groups, but building more homes. This way, starters, seniors, and low-income people get housing faster and status holders ultimately benefit too. Fair and sustainable.

109004.explanation: Nieuw Elan supports as much local energy generation as possible. However, not with wind turbines, which we believe belong at sea.

109005.explanation: Nieuw Elan therefore, for example, supports adding a large sports hall at the sports park.

109007.explanation: The municipality must foster a vital society. The healthy choice should therefore also be the easiest. Nieuw Elan is not opposed to current providers but believes that supply is sufficient.

109008.explanation: Unaffordable owner-occupied homes and long waiting lists are unacceptable. Nieuw Elan wants a balanced housing market with diverse housing types for all life stages. On average, 30 percent social housing in Alphen aan den Rijn is fine, with room for tailored solutions per location for a healthy mix.

109010.explanation: Nieuw Elan believes the municipality should not be too restrictive in this regard. Attention is needed, however, for 'slumlording' in residential neighborhoods, where rooms are repeatedly rented out short-term as if they were a kind of hotel for labor migrants.

109012.explanation: Nieuw Elan supports and has initiated the retention of the first hour and a half of free parking in the city center.

109013.explanation: In most cases, there is sufficient parking available. Where this is not the case, tailored solutions should be developed in consultation with residents to arrive at an appropriate solution.

109014.explanation: The center must remain accessible to everyone. Making it car-free is not the solution. Bicycle use should be encouraged, which we already do in Alphen.

109015.explanation: Nieuw Elan supports greening and better use of nature. Therefore, we are already investing in this with the municipality. According to Nieuw Elan, this applies not only to Boskoop but to the entire municipality.

109017.explanation: Nieuw Elan supports exploring with owners how vacant buildings could possibly be used for housing, cultural purposes, etc. The municipality must embrace any initiatives in this regard.

109020.explanation: The preliminary injunction judge ruled that the Alphense measures for closure were not permitted. Therefore, a reasonable solution must be sought with residents and the adjoining municipality of Nieuwkoop.

109021.explanation: Humane housing for labor migrants working for Boskoop entrepreneurs must remain possible. However, housing labor migrants not working in Boskoop should not be allowed. Consultation with Greenpoort Boskoop is essential.

109023.explanation: These cameras are necessary and proportionate for enforcement and possible investigation by the police.

108955.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stands for equality, inclusion, and equal opportunities in governance and politics. Balanced representation fits with the ambition for a diverse and just local democracy.

108956.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA strongly emphasizes participation, early involvement, and residents having a say. Referenda are not explicitly mentioned, but the principle is that residents have structural influence on important decisions.

108961.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to provide support or land to companies that rely on low-paid labor migrants and require much space and traffic, such as distribution centers.

108966.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA makes no explicit statement on this. The emphasis, however, is on health, safety, and limiting nuisance.

108967.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants bulky waste collected free of charge once a month to encourage waste separation and prevent illegal dumping.

108969.explanation: Accessibility is a core value. The municipality must actively resolve bottlenecks and make buildings accessible for people with disabilities.

108971.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA emphasizes the importance of culture, information provision, and local connectedness.

108979.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stresses social safety and prevention, not further arming enforcement officers.

108980.explanation: Starters receive support through affordable housing and financial instruments.

108986.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opts for tailored solutions and trust, not compulsory counter-performances.

108988.explanation: The threshold is raised to 130 percent of the social minimum.

108994.explanation: In healthcare, we work with experienced experts from diverse communities, so care and support better align with everyone’s background and life world.

108995.explanation: In the case of youth care, quality takes precedence over cost.

108997.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses for stable and fair finances, not for generic tax cuts.

108998.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA uses the proceeds, among other things, for dog walking areas.

109000.explanation: The focus is on better utilization and sustainability of existing sites. Quality over quantity.

109002.explanation: Vulnerable groups, including status holders, receive tailored support and extra assistance with housing.

109004.explanation: Our preference is to combine solar energy with agriculture, nature, or water storage.

109005.explanation: Investing in sustainable, accessible sports facilities for everyone.

109007.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA has no specific position on this. The focus is on prevention, health, and safety.

109008.explanation: This is a strict standard in the election program to tackle the housing shortage.

109010.explanation: We do support a tough approach to slumlords and poor housing quality.

109012.explanation: The inner city will be low-traffic; pricing policy must support livability.

109013.explanation: More focus on shared mobility, cycling, and public transport; parking managed smartly and neighborhood-oriented.

109014.explanation: A low-traffic inner city improves livability, safety, and space for pedestrians and cyclists.

109015.explanation: Nature development, greenery in neighborhoods, and strengthening the landscape are spearheads.

109017.explanation: Active protection and restoration of heritage is explicit policy.

109020.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA would like to see this road closed to benefit a safe cycling connection.

109021.explanation: Labor migrants deserve decent housing; exploitation will be combated.

109023.explanation: Safety is important, but always with attention to proportionality and prevention.

108955.explanation: Parties determine their own input in a coalition, with VVD prioritizing quality.

108956.explanation: The VVD believes that elected council members should make decisions for the municipality. Additionally, a referendum is expensive, and turnout is often low, making the results unrepresentative.

108961.explanation: The municipality of Alphen aan den Rijn is actually one of the locations for large space users in distribution.

108966.explanation: A show as part of community spirit and the desire to celebrate something together as a community. And, a fireworks show can also be done with more modern means than (just) fireworks.

108967.explanation: The current options to deliver bulky waste for free several times at the waste station, or to have it collected at low cost, are sufficient.

108969.explanation: Making it more accessible is also a form of personal hospitality and responsibility of the entrepreneur.

108971.explanation: It is understandable that a municipality supports a local broadcaster from an information perspective. The VVD is not in favor of extra subsidies.

108979.explanation: Besides a bodycam, a baton provides safety for enforcement officers, but it also has a deterrent effect on troublemakers.

108980.explanation: Financial support for housing is a task of the national government.

108986.explanation: The VVD believes that people on social assistance benefits have a responsibility to give something back to society. By doing socially useful work, people stay active, learn new skills, and contribute to society.

108988.explanation: Poverty is defined. The income threshold is defined as well.

108994.explanation: Healthcare in the Netherlands is provided regardless of the person’s background. Care and other assistance must be neutral and remain so.

108995.explanation: The VVD assumes the principle that all organizations provide the same high quality of care.

108997.explanation: The VVD is fundamentally in favor of keeping every form of tax as low as possible.

108998.explanation: A completely pointless tax. According to the VVD, the revenue from the dog tax often does not outweigh the costs of collecting and monitoring it.

109000.explanation: We are running up against limited possibilities, partly because not every area is available for every company anymore.

109002.explanation: The VVD believes social rental homes must be distributed fairly. Everyone on a waiting list should have an equal chance. Refugees must not automatically be given priority over other residents.

109004.explanation: As far as the municipality is concerned, placing solar panels on agricultural land should only be possible with multi-use of the land.

109005.explanation: Renewing the sports parks in Park Zegersloot can only take place if renewal or renovation is really necessary.

109007.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality must seek a balance between the interests of residents and entrepreneurs. New coffee shops may only open if owners comply with the rules and public order is not disturbed. Customized solutions from the municipality are needed here.

109008.explanation: The VVD considers building according to need more important, in which the need for affordable owner-occupied homes seems underestimated with this position.

109010.explanation: Many more housing seekers can be helped through room rentals, which can contribute to alleviating the housing shortage.

109012.explanation: Paid parking with the first hour and a half free in garages works well. For residents of the center, however, this must not lead to higher on-street parking costs. Free parking for residents with a permit is therefore an option.

109013.explanation: Life is already busy enough. Endless searching for a parking spot leads to unnecessary frustration. The VVD therefore believes parking must be well arranged for residents. There must be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods so that everyone can easily park their car.

109014.explanation: The shopping area in the center of the Alphen core is already largely car-free during opening hours. That is enough for the VVD.

109015.explanation: The VVD values green and nature, but the municipality must also consider space for homes and businesses.

109017.explanation: The VVD considers the preservation of cultural heritage important, but the municipality must use tax money wisely. Restoration of the roof tile factory can only take place if it is financially feasible and clearly beneficial for the municipality.

109020.explanation: Alphen aan den Rijn should not close this main road but should make it safer for cyclists.

109021.explanation: There is not yet any (municipally) planned housing but an idea from a developer. For future municipal plans, the VVD believes the scale must be carefully considered, wherever it is.

109023.explanation: Permanent supervision is necessary for the lasting safety of visitors, enforcement officers, and residents.

108955.explanation: Quality must come first; women and men can contribute equally to this, but not by naming a number in advance.

108956.explanation: This can already be done through citizen surveys; a referendum adds nothing. It only creates confusion about who is responsible for what.

108961.explanation: It is not necessary to accommodate any more large office blocks in our municipality, especially if this creates little employment and only increases traffic congestion.

108966.explanation: Given the national fireworks ban, alternatives must be possible, preferably in cooperation with other organizations.

108967.explanation: We prefer the current policy, whereby the 'polluter' also partly pays.

108969.explanation: That is the entrepreneur's own responsibility.

108971.explanation: The municipality already does this; a decision has recently been made on this with our support.

108979.explanation: Enforcement officers play an important role in safety and livability. With appropriate powers, they can act more quickly against nuisances, complementing the police and prevention efforts.

108980.explanation: The CDA wants to introduce starter loans to give young households access to the housing market.

108986.explanation: Through participation, customization, and guidance, not through obligations that may backfire.

108988.explanation: This is already taken into account. Customization must be possible to keep support accessible for working people who have difficulty making ends meet.

108994.explanation: We believe healthcare should be people-centered. Freedom of choice contributes to trust, appropriate care, and dignity.

108995.explanation: The CDA chooses quality and continuity in youth care. The lowest price must never be leading.

108997.explanation: The CDA values affordable expenses but does not opt for automatic reductions in charges. We aim for a financially healthy municipality, maintaining facilities and a multi-year balanced budget. Only if this is responsible can a reduction in charges be considered.

108998.explanation: The CDA supports abolishing the dog tax. The lost revenue will be compensated in the multi-year budget.

109000.explanation: The CDA wants room for entrepreneurs and jobs close to home. We support new business parks only if genuinely necessary, well integrated, and fitting with accessibility, landscape, and livability. Currently, much business park space remains undeveloped and should be utilized first.

109002.explanation: The CDA adheres to the legal task of housing status holders but advocates equal treatment in the housing market. Since the cabinet wants to abolish priority in social rent, we focus on housing through alternatives such as shared housing, flexible homes, and private rentals.

109004.explanation: The CDA prioritizes solar panels on roofs. Agricultural land is intended for food production and landscape.

109005.explanation: We invest in good sports facilities because sport contributes to health, social interaction, and prevention. In Zegersloot park, the boot camp areas and playground have recently all been renewed, so we prefer to invest elsewhere.

109007.explanation: We want to prevent nuisance and crime. Maintaining the current situation is our preference.

109008.explanation: The CDA wants sufficient social rental housing and finds a 35 percent share appropriate for new construction. A minimum of 40 percent is too high because middle incomes like starters just above the social rental threshold also need housing. Therefore, we aim for a balanced housing mix.

109010.explanation: The CDA wants to tackle abuses in housing for roomers and labor migrants and prevent nuisance. We support a ban on unregulated room rental by landlords, coupled with licensing requirements and enforcement, so that living remains safe, fair, and habitable for neighborhoods.

109012.explanation: The CDA wants an accessible center, but parking fees must fit livability and parking pressure on streets for residents. Therefore, paid parking in the Castellum parking garage remains free for the first hour and a half.

109013.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce parking pressure in residential neighborhoods. Where there is congestion, we create space for extra spots with attention to safety and greenery. In new neighborhoods like Gnephoek, this can be done well at the edge of the built-up area.

109014.explanation: The CDA wants a center that remains accessible for everyone. Reducing car traffic and greening are possible; a fully car-free center goes too far for us.

109015.explanation: The CDA wants not only Boskoop but also the other villages and Alphen to become greener and healthier. More greenery helps against heat stress and increases livability.

109017.explanation: Since it is not owned by Alphen, we cannot enforce this. The CDA wants to preserve heritage and make a new destination possible. We support restoration if there is a feasible plan.

109020.explanation: If access for destination traffic and emergency services is well arranged, the CDA wants to structurally address cut-through traffic and unsafe situations on Ziendeweg. Closing it to car traffic improves traffic safety and livability.

109021.explanation: The CDA wants decent housing with strict quality requirements and enforcement. Ignoring means looking away; we choose to regulate and monitor.

109023.explanation: The CDA supports temporary camera surveillance if necessary. Permanent surveillance is only appropriate in exceptional necessity; we want to continue evaluating.

108955.explanation: We strive for a good mix of people, including gender balance as part of it.

108956.explanation: A referendum is not always a good choice for complex topics. D66 aims to use a broad range of methods, including citizens' assemblies with randomly selected citizens participating.

108961.explanation: Distribution centers cause relatively much (traffic) nuisance and provide little employment.

108966.explanation: The municipality does not need to organize this itself but should facilitate potential initiatives.

108967.explanation: The current arrangement allowing bulky waste to be collected free a few times is sufficient.

108969.explanation: This is the responsibility of the entrepreneur. The municipality can advise the entrepreneur. The municipality is responsible for its own buildings.

108971.explanation: An independent news supply is important.

108979.explanation: There is no reason to equip enforcement officers with batons. The monopoly on violence lies with the police.

108980.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

108986.explanation: We find it important that residents contribute. However, the municipality can support and encourage citizens but not force them.

108988.explanation: D66 believes residents with low incomes should receive extra support. Raising the threshold does not solve the problem.

108995.explanation: It concerns the effectiveness of the intervention/support.

108997.explanation: Given the municipality’s financial situation, it cannot afford this without it affecting facilities.

108998.explanation: The proceeds must benefit facilities for dogs.

109000.explanation: The number of business parks is too limited, especially given the municipality’s growth.

109002.explanation: Refugees have unequal opportunities because they could not register. Moreover, this means the asylum centers remain full, causing other problems.

109004.explanation: At the same time, good use of public space must be considered.

109005.explanation: Not every sports park needs renewal. Only invest where necessary.

109007.explanation: There are enough coffeeshops in Alphen.

109008.explanation: D66 pursues the norm of 30 percent to keep housing construction profitable.

109010.explanation: D66 aims for creative methods to solve the housing problem. We cannot afford to disregard options. This must not lead to nuisance, and whole houses must not be converted solely for room rental.

109013.explanation: The current parking balance is fine; we aim for more sustainable and healthy forms of mobility.

109014.explanation: The city center must also remain accessible to cars.

109015.explanation: D66 strives for a green municipality.

109017.explanation: We have no opinion on this.

109020.explanation: D66 prioritizes the bicycle. There are good alternatives to cars.

109021.explanation: Good policies must be developed ensuring housing matches the needs Alphen aan den Rijn has for labor migrants.

109023.explanation: This infringement on privacy must be used in a targeted manner.

108955.explanation: It's about the quality of the aldermen, not whether they are men or women.

108956.explanation: Both corrective and advisory referendums should be possible, but only when they are clearly and independently organized, the questions are clear and neutral, and the information provided is objective and complete.

108961.explanation: Alphen already has many distribution centers which take up a lot of space and basically provide little employment. Furthermore, these building blocks spoil the landscape.

108966.explanation: Given the legislation, all fireworks are now prohibited, so the municipality cannot organize a fireworks show. Besides, this is not a municipal task.

108967.explanation: The current rates are very affordable, and there is no such thing as free service because costs are always incurred and therefore charged through the waste collection fee.

108969.explanation: This is the responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality should only ensure that public buildings are accessible.

108971.explanation: The local broadcaster is essential for a well-informed community and deserves support, maintaining full independence.

108979.explanation: BOS officers must undergo special training for this, and batons may only be used in exceptional situations.

108980.explanation: We agree with this, but these loans are provided by the Housing Stimulus Fund for Dutch Municipalities, and the municipality only determines eligibility.

108986.explanation: If people are capable of this and it indeed concerns social work, this is not a problem, and it may help them re-enter the labor process.

108988.explanation: There should be poverty policies, but not solely based on income. Due to all exemptions and allowances, low-income residents often receive more money than those just above the threshold who are eligible for nothing.

108994.explanation: Considering the major shortage of healthcare staff, people should be grateful there is care available. Only then can it be considered whether the desired caregiver who suits the individual is present.

108995.explanation: It is primarily about proven quality.

108997.explanation: A smaller, more efficient organization that delivers quality work at reasonable costs, focusing on core matters without unnecessary rules or costly enforcement. Hiring external staff should be minimized and must be justifiable both substantively and financially.

108998.explanation: Dog tax only if it is spent on dog policy and not added to general funds. Otherwise, abolish it.

109000.explanation: Partly these industrial estates should be developed around the transshipment terminal. Old industrial estates should be cleaned up for transformation into housing.

109002.explanation: We have people who have been on the housing waiting list for years, often living with their parents. It is incomprehensible why status holders should be given priority.

109004.explanation: Priority goes to solar panels on roofs.

109005.explanation: In our opinion, there is currently no reason for that.

109007.explanation: The current coffeeshops reportedly cause little nuisance. We do not know whether there is still demand for more coffeeshops.

109008.explanation: We want at least 35 percent social housing, 35 percent mid-rent/mid-buy. This distribution improves the flow of home seekers.

109010.explanation: If a maximum of four rooms are rented to a maximum of four people, this type can offer a solution for the housing market unless there is nuisance or exploitation of tenants.

109012.explanation: Yes, but this must not lead to an increase in parking costs for residents in the city center.

109013.explanation: Creating more parking spaces prevents nuisance. Especially in old neighborhoods, more attention must be given to creating parking bays during any neighborhood redesign.

109014.explanation: RijnGouweLokaal is firmly against this. Both shopkeepers and residents in this area will be the victims of a car-free zone.

109015.explanation: There is already enough greenery in Boskoop, except in the center which is now being addressed.

109017.explanation: This location is perfectly suitable for residential development.

109020.explanation: There is no reason to close this road off, and the municipality was ruled against by the court.

109021.explanation: More than five hundred labor migrants is too many for this preferred location. This site is too large-scale for the existing environment.

109023.explanation: Camera surveillance at least provides extra monitoring and hopefully safety.

108955.explanation: Good governance is formed by people with a strong moral and integrity compass. Local leaders must be held accountable for this. The new board of mayor and aldermen must include officials who can handle the task: the right people in the right places.

108956.explanation: Referendums are not a solution for complex political issues. Furthermore, advisory referendum results are not binding, which causes confusion and dissatisfaction. A (broad) survey is a better alternative.

108961.explanation: The municipality does not allow further 'concreting' of the rural area by preventing new solitary distribution centers in our green area, except for the expansion of existing industrial sites.

108966.explanation: From 2026 onwards, there will be a nationwide fireworks ban. An organized fireworks show can be experienced beautifully (and digitally) together.

108967.explanation: Proper waste separation must pay off. To give everyone a fair chance, we ensure every household has the right facilities (also for bulky waste). We will provide a solution for diapers and incontinence materials because no one can help that. No more waste, only raw materials.

108969.explanation: The municipality ensures that its buildings, facilities, public spaces, and public transport are accessible to all residents, and digitally the municipality must also be accessible for people with disabilities. Entrepreneurs are expected to do the same.

108971.explanation: There are comprehensive agreements with the local broadcaster.

108979.explanation: We look at obstacles to social safety on the street or in public transport when they form a barrier for people to fully participate. The ability to use a baton is a tool.

108980.explanation: Based on the Christian-social values of solidarity and mercy, we want to advocate in Alphen aan den Rijn for the construction of affordable owner-occupied and rental homes for starters, those with a small budget, and people with care needs.

108986.explanation: Everyone participates! Not only work provides meaning and an active role in society. For example, through volunteer work and (work-related) daytime activities, people also participate fully. The ChristianUnion commits to supporting these residents as well as possible.

108988.explanation: The ChristianUnion is committed to supporting residents as effectively as possible, regardless of the reason they cannot participate: poverty, debts, a disability, and so on.

108994.explanation: We are in favor of 'care tailored to the individual' and for that the personal budget (pgb) is the most suitable instrument.

108995.explanation: The ChristianUnion considers it important that proven assistance methods are used within youth care and favors using an effects monitor to achieve better and appropriate guidance and effective and responsible spending of the available funds.

108997.explanation: If there are no other options, municipal levies, other than indexation, can be increased if necessary to maintain facilities.

108998.explanation: The dog tax contributes to the costs incurred by the municipality.

109000.explanation: The municipality does not allow further 'concreting' of the rural area by preventing new solitary distribution centers in our green area, except for the expansion of existing industrial sites.

109002.explanation: Our municipality also takes responsibility for housing asylum seekers with a residence permit and commits to finding housing for them.

109004.explanation: The development and implementation of solar and wind programs must be done carefully. This requires active involvement of local residents, landowners, and other stakeholders. No solar parks just anywhere on land.

109005.explanation: These were renewed in the period 2010-2018.

109007.explanation: The ChristenUnie opposes the presence of coffee shops and/or the cultivation of cannabis by municipalities.

109008.explanation: We build in all price ranges. This ensures a healthy mix of affordable and more expensive homes that match our municipality's housing desires. We choose a distribution of 40 percent cheap, 40 percent mid-priced, and 20 percent expensive.

109010.explanation: The starting point of the ChristianUnion is always 'buying is living.' In other words, for owner-occupied homes, the ChristianUnion introduces an owner-occupation obligation.

109012.explanation: Introducing paid parking in residential neighborhoods in the city center only happens after careful consultation with residents and only in places where parking problems exist or arise. Customization and a hardship clause are important. There must be an exception for churchgoers on Sundays.

109013.explanation: Parking spaces will consist of semi-paving without compromising the accessibility of public spaces.

109014.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports the introduction of a zero-emission zone in the center of Alphen aan den Rijn, provided conditions are met such as sufficient charging infrastructure and parking options outside the zone for residents living in the area.

109017.explanation: Heritage and monuments are defining elements in municipalities. They are beacons in a changing world and carry memories. We consider proper maintenance and management of heritage and monuments important and also look at new uses for old buildings.

109020.explanation: So far, the municipality has lost the legal procedure. The current council has decided to leave it at that. The ChristianUnion will focus in the coming period on making traffic safer especially for pedestrians and cyclists. Closure is not an end goal in itself.

109021.explanation: Based on the Environmental Act, the municipality creates a program 'Labor migrants housing'. In this, the municipality can include housing criteria for initiators or companies who want to employ labor migrants. The housing must be safe, hygienic, and appropriate (SNF).

109023.explanation: More supervision is necessary in case of nuisance or unsafe situations through, for example, security cameras and patrols.

108955.explanation: The SP has equality as one of its principles.

108956.explanation: A local referendum should be held on weighty matters.

108961.explanation: It is provincial policy to locate distribution centers along A-roads. It's time to provide locations for starting innovative companies. Our municipality is close to knowledge centers in Delft, Leiden, Utrecht, Rotterdam, and Amsterdam.

108966.explanation: There is a nationwide fireworks ban. Almost three-quarters of the Dutch support this ban. So why would the municipality hold a fireworks show?

108967.explanation: Introducing a waste collection levy causes extra littering.

108969.explanation: Continuous investment is necessary in the accessibility and usability of accommodations for people with a disability; extra attention is given to safe and accessible routes to care facilities, supermarkets, and meeting places.

108971.explanation: The local broadcaster is funded through a statutory contribution from the municipal fund, in 2025 €80,000. Local broadcasters can receive additional subsidies in 2026 and 2027 through the Stimulus Fund for Journalism. There are also revenues from advertising.

108979.explanation: We ensure there is sufficient capacity for all tasks that the BOAs (special enforcement officers) have and expand this where necessary. We do not cut budgets and invest extra where needed.

108980.explanation: Having an affordable own home enables people to progress financially faster in life. We will create financing options for starters so they can more easily obtain a home. We will also ensure more starter homes are built.

108986.explanation: Everyone must be able to contribute, but some are unable to do so. We support organizations that help people return to work more quickly.

108988.explanation: We maintain the special assistance threshold at at least 120% of the minimum wage. People earning more than 120% often also live in poverty. They must be helped too. Therefore, the income threshold for assistance must eventually be raised.

108994.explanation: The SP wants to leave no one behind. Good care must be personal, tailored, and available for everyone.

108995.explanation: The SP wants to leave no one behind. Good care must be personal, tailored, and available for everyone. Care is not a market. Market forces in healthcare often reduce quality.

108997.explanation: The possibility of exemption from the property tax (OZB) for those who own a home after a working life but have a low income must be investigated.

108998.explanation: The dog tax should be abolished. If this tax remains, the money should be reserved for animal-related matters.

109000.explanation: It is time to offer the possibility for start-up innovative companies to settle. Our municipality is close to knowledge centers in Delft, Leiden, Utrecht, Rotterdam, and Amsterdam.

109002.explanation: A refugee is not the same as a recognized resident. We ensure that refugees feel as welcome as possible. However, this should not be at the expense of the other residents of our municipality.

109004.explanation: The SP believes sustainability must be affordable for everyone, including people with a small budget, so that every house, flat, and neighborhood can become more beautiful and cleaner. We want to leave the earth better for future generations while keeping it affordable for everyone.

109005.explanation: Sport is important for social contacts and health and should be encouraged. Therefore, good sports facilities must be available.

109007.explanation: The current coffeeshops do not need to disappear. Expansion of their number is not desired. The municipality, police, entrepreneurs, and residents work together on concrete plans to counter any nuisances.

109008.explanation: The requirement for 30% social rental housing in new construction should be increased to 40%. The same percentage should apply jointly for middle-rent and affordable homeownership, as backlogs need to be caught up.

109010.explanation: The SP wants a vacancy ordinance to combat housing speculation and reduce waiting times. Municipal regulations should also be adjusted to prevent the rental of homes that should actually become available for starters and ordinary tenants.

109012.explanation: Parking should even be free as it attracts customers, which benefits entrepreneurs in the center. Therefore, entrepreneurs should bear the costs. Residents of the center are currently the victims of paid parking.

109013.explanation: All neighborhoods must be accessible by all types of transportation.

109014.explanation: The introduction of the zero-emission zone in Alphen and a general speed limit of 30 kilometers per hour for vehicles should be critically re-examined regarding their actual effects.

109015.explanation: Greenery and nature are important for the living environment. Nature areas, parks, and green spaces are prioritized and expanded and improved where possible, including in Boskoop.

109017.explanation: We support a heritage fund and a culture fund. The SP supports the preservation of cultural-historical objects/heritage. This complex falls under the category of monumental buildings.

109020.explanation: Road closures, such as the Ziendeweg between Nieuwkoop/Aarlanderveen and Zwammerdam, should be reconsidered. Ultimately, the closure must be permanently reversed. Detours caused by the closure ultimately lead to higher costs and more traffic.

109021.explanation: The SP was the first to take action against the establishment of labor migrants on Middelburgseweg in Boskoop. Employers using labor migrants will be encouraged to provide housing mostly on their own premises.

109023.explanation: Camera surveillance is desirable when it is aimed at reducing crime. Think of street violence in the nightlife area or specific problem spots in our municipality.

108955.explanation: The SGP focuses on the substantive quality of the municipal executive. As far as we are concerned, that is what should be evaluated. The council should reflect society as much as possible as a representative body; the executive mainly has implementing tasks.

108956.explanation: The municipal council is the highest body in the municipality and is elected to represent the citizens. It is good to consult residents on important issues, but the final judgment lies with the council.

108961.explanation: There is a lot of activity in logistics in Alphen aan den Rijn. A new distribution center also attracts other service providers. This is important for employment. However, it is important that each application is assessed for added value and fit within the neighborhood.

108966.explanation: The SGP does not see organizing such a show as a municipal task and knows of better destinations for tax money. The municipality's role is mainly facilitative toward initiators.

108967.explanation: The collection of bulky waste belongs to the municipal resource policy. Waste collection must remain cost-efficient and accessible. The waste tax is cost-covering.

108969.explanation: The entrepreneur is responsible for making their building accessible. The municipality can play a supportive role in this.

108971.explanation: The SGP recognizes the importance of the local broadcaster. To maintain these broadcasters, a contribution from the national government is especially desired. Otherwise, the local broadcaster must report on the administration on which they financially depend.

108979.explanation: The tasks of enforcement officers (boa's) are clearly defined and within their specific powers. Police tasks remain within the police's authority. If boa's receive heavier responsibilities by exception, their equipment must be adapted accordingly.

108980.explanation: With a starter loan, a young person has a greater chance of buying a house.

108986.explanation: A contribution may be expected from people receiving social assistance benefits if they are capable. A great advantage could be that people build more social contacts and feel more valued.

108988.explanation: The income limit currently set is already above the national standard.

108994.explanation: By offering a personal budget (pgb), it should be possible to choose care that suits you. Being able to choose care that aligns with your faith, culture, or beliefs promotes your well-being.

108995.explanation: When selecting organizations, the overall picture matters. This means price certainly plays a role, but the offered quality must be adequate.

108997.explanation: The municipality’s financial situation is not very generous. A reduction in property tax (ozb) means cuts elsewhere. It must be avoided that too drastic cuts put facilities under pressure.

108998.explanation: Off-leash areas are being developed for dogs and various facilities are available. Unfortunately, money is also needed to counter the nuisance of dog poop, as not all dog owners take responsibility. A tax for this is acceptable.

109000.explanation: Business parks are necessary for a healthy economy of the municipality. It is important that there is sufficient space for this. If needed, the SGP is not against more business parks.

109002.explanation: Priority groups do not have priority over existing waiting lists for the municipality’s own residents. If the needs of priority groups cannot be sufficiently met, we focus on alternative and temporary housing.

109004.explanation: Although it is not the SGP’s preference, the municipality may permit solar panels on agricultural land. It is also important that preliminary research is done to determine if there is enough support.

109005.explanation: The municipality is responsible for maintaining sports parks. This includes necessary renewal.

109007.explanation: The SGP favors a strict drug policy by the municipality that does not allow coffeeshops.

109008.explanation: The SGP supports 30 percent social housing in new construction. By building more, more social housing can be created. However, this should not come at the expense of other groups who earn too much for social housing.

109010.explanation: Rooms are mainly rented by young people who often study elsewhere. Renting a room is the only affordable option. The municipality may enforce rules on abuses (which unfortunately also occur in migrant housing) but should not prohibit it.

109012.explanation: Paid parking costs the municipality money. Making parking cheaper means the municipality must cut elsewhere or increase taxes.

109013.explanation: If possible, additional parking spaces should only be added if there is a demonstrable lack of parking. In most neighborhoods, this is not the case.

109014.explanation: Residents should be able to access their homes wherever possible, and parking garages must remain accessible. Expansion of the car-free zone will only be considered if it adds value.

109015.explanation: A green living environment improves quality of life. Where possible, greenery should be added instead of paving.

109017.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to restore the factory; a new purpose is likely more promising.

109020.explanation: Ziendeweg is not suitable for the traffic currently using it. However, it is positive that better decision-making is taking place.

109021.explanation: Labor migrants play an important role in our economy, but their housing often does not meet standards. Companies and the municipality must ensure an acceptable quality of living for both labor migrants and the immediate surroundings.

109023.explanation: As long as camera surveillance is a meaningful addition, these cameras may continue to be used.

108955.explanation: FVD disagrees with this statement because quality and suitability are more important than gender. When forming a council, experience, knowledge, and administrative skills should be the primary focus. A mandatory equal division could actually have a counterproductive effect.

108956.explanation: FVD agrees with this statement because a referendum directly involves residents in important decisions. This gives citizens more influence over policies that directly affect them. This strengthens democracy: FVD originated from this principle.

108961.explanation: Distribution centers provide employment opportunities for residents of the municipality. They offer jobs at various levels, which can strengthen the local economy. Additionally, distribution centers generate revenue for the municipality, for example through taxes and collaboration with local businesses.

108966.explanation: FVD does not think it is wise to spend public money on a fireworks show. That money can be better used for more important matters, such as healthcare, safety, or maintenance of facilities. We oppose a fireworks ban: freedom must be preserved to celebrate New Year's Eve.

108967.explanation: 'Free' bulky waste collection prevents littering and illegal dumping. If people can easily dispose of bulky waste, streets and neighborhoods remain cleaner. However, it is important to limit this to a maximum of six times per year.

108969.explanation: It is the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves to make their buildings accessible. Accessibility is part of good entrepreneurship and should not be the municipality's task. Furthermore, it is unfair to use public funds for modifications to private buildings.

108971.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to provide extra funding to a local broadcaster. The local broadcaster should operate as independently as possible and seek other funding sources. The municipality can support the local broadcaster through cooperation and facilities.

108979.explanation: Enforcement officers increasingly face aggression and unsafe situations. To do their work properly and safely, they must have sufficient defensive tools. In addition to a baton, pepper spray can be a suitable means, as it allows officers to respond from a distance.

108980.explanation: A starter loan can drive up housing prices. Additional financial support increases demand for homes. Moreover, it is unfair to use public funds for a specific group of homebuyers.

108986.explanation: Socially meaningful work provides structure and routine and keeps people actively involved in society. Additionally, this work contributes to valuable projects such as neighborhood help, green maintenance, or support for social organizations. This way, the benefit also produces results.

108988.explanation: There are many working poor who have a job but still struggle to make ends meet. Because of the current income threshold, they often fall outside assistance programs, even though they urgently need help. People who work and earn just above the threshold should not be worse off.

108994.explanation: It is important that healthcare is professional, respectful, and of good quality for everyone. Personal preferences can be considered where possible, but cannot always be leading. Furthermore, unlimited choice can lead to inequality and higher costs.

108995.explanation: In youth care, quality and continuity are more important than the lowest price. Vulnerable children and families need good and reliable help. If the municipality only focuses on price, this can lead to more problems and higher costs in the long term.

108997.explanation: It is wiser to maintain the property tax (ozb) at current levels so the municipality can continue to perform its tasks well. Increases due to improper governance must be made public.

108998.explanation: Dog tax is outdated. Many municipalities have already abolished this tax as it is not a purpose tax. Moreover, it is unfair that only dog owners pay extra tax, while owners of other pets do not.

109000.explanation: If there is a real need. Business parks provide employment and strengthen the local economy. However, the municipality must carefully consider where and how many business parks are necessary.

109002.explanation: It is unfair to housing seekers who have waited for a social rental home for many years. Many residents have been registered for sometimes ten years or more without priority. This priority also increases pressure on the already very tight social housing market.

109004.explanation: Agricultural land is intended for food production. Installing solar panels reduces farmland available to farmers and can put food supply under pressure. Additionally, changing the use of agricultural land alters the landscape and nature.

109005.explanation: The current sports parks still function adequately, and large-scale renovation is not necessary. Maintenance where needed is often sufficient. The municipality should therefore use public funds carefully and only invest if renewal is truly necessary.

109007.explanation: Additional coffee shops can lead to more nuisance and safety issues in residential areas and the center, such as crowds and drug tourism.

109008.explanation: Forty percent social housing is too high. Nationally, about 28 percent of the housing stock is social rental, so a mandated 40 percent is disproportionate. We advocate for a more balanced share of 30 percent social housing, allowing room for mid-rent housing as well.

109010.explanation: Renting out individual rooms is important for students, starters, and people looking for temporary housing. Without room rentals, it becomes even harder for these groups to find affordable housing. Moreover, room rental offers more flexibility in a tight housing market.

109012.explanation: Paid parking currently forms a barrier for visitors to the center. By making the first two hours free, the center becomes more accessible to shoppers and visitors. This stimulates the local economy and businesses.

109013.explanation: In many neighborhoods, parking pressure is a major problem. Residents increasingly struggle to park their cars near home. Additionally, the number of cars is increasing while parking spaces often do not grow accordingly. More parking spaces reduce nuisance and frustration.

109014.explanation: A completely car-free center reduces accessibility for residents, the elderly, and entrepreneurs. However, we do think it is sensible to make the shopping area car-free. This increases safety, calm, and space for pedestrians.

109015.explanation: More parks and nature create a healthier and more pleasant living environment. Greenery offers space for relaxation, exercise, and social interaction. Boskoop has traditionally been a nursery village where greenery thrives.

109017.explanation: The Nieuw Werklust roof tile factory is important industrial heritage for Hazerswoude-Rijndijk and the municipality of Alphen aan den Rijn. The building tells an important part of local history. Costs should be charged to the owner.

109020.explanation: Permanently closing the Ziendeweg worsens accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and emergency services.

109021.explanation: The planned housing places too much pressure on Boskoop. The village already faces limited space and facilities. There is also insufficient support among residents. Decisions of this magnitude must take the livability and concerns of neighbors into account.

109023.explanation: Because camera surveillance should remain temporary and only be used when truly necessary. Permanent surveillance infringes on the privacy of residents and visitors. Moreover, it is not proven that permanent camera surveillance structurally leads to greater safety.

108997.explanation: The property tax (ozb) should be used to maintain facilities.

109008.explanation: We support 30 percent social rental housing.

108955.explanation: The daily executive board should reflect the population (with a ratio between 40-60 percent). The Party for the Animals supports a sufficiently inclusive and diverse public administration.

108956.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports a consultative referendum on subjects that are relevant to a large portion of the residents.

108961.explanation: Distribution centers take up a lot of space, cause extra mobility, and hardly provide any employment.

108966.explanation: Fireworks are outdated. Now that the traditional consumer fireworks are banned from this year onwards, alternative entertainment must be sought. Polluting fireworks no longer fit into this.

108967.explanation: Everyone can drop off bulky household waste sorted free of charge at the municipality's collection depot. Collection at home incurs disproportionately high costs for the community.

108969.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes that all publicly accessible spaces must be accessible to people with disabilities. However, entrepreneurs should bear the costs themselves and not the community.

108971.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes that local broadcasters deserve support to keep residents informed about developments within the municipality.

108979.explanation: Safety comes first, also for the enforcement officers themselves, for their protection. Society is becoming increasingly aggressive and supervisors must be equipped accordingly.

108980.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to help first-time homebuyers. Housing is seen as a basic right and first-time buyers must have a chance in the housing market. A starter loan fits the aim of affordable housing for everyone.

108986.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes mandatory reciprocal obligations. Volunteering and caregiving are supported, but not made compulsory. The benefit recipient is central, not the free deployment of labor.

108988.explanation: Indeed, the Party for the Animals raises the income limit to 130 percent of the social minimum for poverty schemes.

108994.explanation: People should be able to choose tailored care. Care providers and doctors must take into account the background, identity, and lifestyle of clients.

108995.explanation: The Party for the Animals stands for quality in youth care. Price should never be leading here.

108997.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to invest in people, animals, and nature. Therefore, property tax cannot be lowered. Quality of life for people and animals is not determined by budget cuts on property tax, but by making better choices in municipal spending.

108998.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to abolish unfair dog taxes as long as these funds are not used solely for the welfare of dogs.

109000.explanation: The Party for the Animals prefers restructuring over expansion of outdated or troublesome industrial areas. It wants to make industrial estates circular and sustainable or convert them into residential areas with plenty of greenery, instead of creating new industrial estates.

109002.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports the current policy of housing status holders.

109004.explanation: The Party for the Animals follows the 'solar ladder', so first solar panels on roofs, only as a last option on agricultural land. Agricultural land is for (plant-based) food production and nature, not for large-scale solar parks.

109005.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes sports parks should have a modern, sustainable, and green appearance. Exercising in 'green' spaces is more relaxing than in urban environments. Ecological management of Zegersloot sports park can greatly improve the biodiversity in the park.

109007.explanation: The Party for the Animals opts for a health-oriented approach over repression. It is alert to drug use but wants enforcement to primarily focus on hard drugs and illegal trade.

109008.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants a larger supply of social rental homes. The share of more than 40 percent social rental housing aligns with our ideal of public housing.

109010.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to tackle abuses in room rental (slumlords), but not impose a total ban. Students and young people should be able to live in rooms.

109012.explanation: The Party for the Animals discourages car parking in the center and encourages cycling and public transport. The current paid parking rates in the center are already very low compared to neighboring cities.

109013.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants more greenery in neighborhoods instead of parking spaces. The standard for parking spaces per inhabitant should be lowered to especially not stimulate the purchase of a second car.

109014.explanation: The center of Alphen aan den Rijn is already car-free. The Party for the Animals supports expanding car-free centers in the surrounding villages.

109015.explanation: Boskoop is already very green. When cultural parcels become available, it should be considered whether conversion to park or nature is possible.

109017.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes this cultural heritage must not be lost. The municipality of Alphen aan den Rijn was known for its many roof tile factories along the Oude Rijn. The last visible remnants must be preserved.

109020.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes the road along a Natura 2000 area (Nieuwkoopse Plassen) should be closed to motorized traffic because many animals, including the rare fish otter, have been demonstrably killed there. It also becomes safer for cyclists and pedestrians.

109021.explanation: The Party for the Animals finds that sufficient adequate housing is arranged for labor migrants in the municipality of Alphen aan den Rijn. Especially in Boskoop, where much seasonal work takes place, foreign workers have a right to good housing.

109023.explanation: The Party for the Animals is against structural camera surveillance for privacy reasons unless circumstances require it. Given the number of violent incidents on Hooftstraat and Julianastraat, structural camera surveillance is a welcome addition to the enforcement tools of supervisors and police.

108955.explanation: Being a man or woman should not make a difference. The right person in the right place. That is what matters.

108956.explanation: Residents have elected a municipal council. These council members should conscientiously represent the interests of their voters.

108961.explanation: Alphen aan den Rijn is located in the center of the Green Heart. This means that logistically we have a very favorable location for distribution centers.

108966.explanation: If we want to give residents an enjoyable New Year's Eve after the fireworks ban is implemented, this could be a nice solution.

108967.explanation: The current system works fine in itself. If you want to bring it yourself, you can do so quickly. If you want bulky waste collected, a small fee is acceptable.

108969.explanation: Entrepreneurs are perfectly capable of determining how to arrange their building and will take accessibility into account. People with disabilities are after all just customers too.

108971.explanation: In the recent period, the municipality has also made an additional contribution available for our local broadcaster.

108979.explanation: Supervisors must be able to have a baton for their own safety. Of course, with the proper training.

108980.explanation: The municipality must not interfere with banks. Banks have the knowledge and expertise for this. Municipalities should stay far away from this.

108986.explanation: If this is possible within the means of the benefit recipient, it is only good. Volunteering gives you rhythm and a network, which only increases the chance of re-entering the workforce.

108988.explanation: There is a large group of working people who just miss out on all waivers and subsidies. As a result, they often end up with less money than people who are on benefits. They too have the right to assistance when poverty threatens.

108994.explanation: The task of any healthcare provider is to empathize with the client. Furthermore, it is good that organizations take into account religion, culture, or belief systems.

108995.explanation: The municipality must choose the most effective form of assistance. What results have the providers achieved? Tailor-made solutions are the key here.

108997.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is an important source of municipal income. However, the municipality should not recklessly increase property tax rates due to financial setbacks. Spending differently and budget cuts within your own organization should be your first priority.

108998.explanation: As long as the dog tax is used to plug budget holes and ends up in the general funds, it is an unfair tax. These revenues do not benefit dogs, pets, or animal welfare.

109000.explanation: Better organizing the current industrial estates can yield huge gains. Counteracting the fragmentation of industrial areas will also make a difference. Make optimal use of the available space.

109002.explanation: The same rules must apply to everyone when allocating social rental housing. The municipality has options to grant urgency in urgent cases.

109004.explanation: Placing solar panels on agricultural land is an excellent way to provide more affordable energy. If farmers want to cooperate, that is fine.

109005.explanation: Sports parks must be properly maintained so that everyone who needs it can engage in sports in a good way. We should only renovate where necessary.

109007.explanation: In our opinion, the supply of coffeeshops is sufficient. As long as nuisance around these coffeeshops is not adequately controlled, we see no reason for further expansion.

109008.explanation: At least 30 percent of the homes to be built in Alphen aan den Rijn should be social rental housing. To make up for lost ground for starters, it is desirable that a large part of the homes to be built fall into this category.

109010.explanation: Renting rooms, provided it is well regulated, need not be a problem. Housing young people, people going through a divorce, and labor migrants is already a problem and can take place properly in this way.

109012.explanation: A large part of the parking fee is used to finance the one and a half hours of free parking. We will still 'enjoy' this for another year. After that, parking fees must return to a normal level.

109013.explanation: There are neighborhoods within Alphen where there is too little parking space, causing motorists to park on sidewalks and the like. This creates undesirable situations. People with wheelchairs can no longer pass. So where parking problems are greatest, address them responsibly.

109014.explanation: A large part of the center is already car-free. From our point of view, there is no need to expand this zone further.

109015.explanation: More greenery in our villages and centers enhances living pleasure.

109017.explanation: The roof tile factory is a national monument. Due to its dilapidated condition, it is now unsafe and adds nothing to the environment. By restoring the site and buildings, it can once again become a prominent part of Hazerswoude.

109020.explanation: No research has shown that closing the Ziendeweg contributes to traffic safety or environmental improvement.

109021.explanation: Large-scale housing of labor migrants in the outskirts of Boskoop is inviting problems.

109023.explanation: Camera surveillance in entertainment and risk areas can increase the sense of safety. Moreover, it can simplify the detection of offenders.

statements.title: The temporary camera surveillance in Hooftstraat and Julianastraat should become permanent.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: in de Hooftstraat en Julianastraat moet blijvend worden.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.

titleParts.text: moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Alphen aan den Rijn should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Alphen aan den Rijn controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
At least 40 percent of newly built homes in Alphen aan den Rijn should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands that is currently 28 percent.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Alphen aan den Rijn moet minimaal 40 procent

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is dat nu 28 procent.

statements.title: Residents of Alphen aan den Rijn should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Alphen aan den Rijn moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Alphen aan den Rijn should renovate the sports parks in Zegersloot Park.
More parks and natural areas should be created in Boskoop.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in Alphen aan den Rijn.
The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should abandon the planned housing for migrant workers in Boskoop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet afzien van de geplande huisvesting voor

titleParts.glossary: People who have come from another country to the Netherlands to work here.

statements.title: The center of Alphen should become car-free.
Alphen should have the Nieuw Werklust roof-tile factory in Hazerswoude-Rijndijk restored.
There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Alphen should permanently close Ziendeweg to motor vehicle traffic.
